summary: A Go-based monolithic CLI tool featuring an MCP integration layer, a schema validation engine, and a test runner, heavily utilizing automated agent-driven test generation.
concerns: High volume of auto-generated extra tests (GOrchestra/glm-agents) may bloat the codebase
Untracked and unstaged files in git status suggest incomplete commits or workflow issues
Stale handoff prompts being marked completed indicates possible process lag in agent orchestration
Heavy reliance on test files for core logic understanding suggests a need for better documentation
suggestions: Consolidate or archive the GOrchestra agent-generated test directories to reduce clutter
Resolve the current git status (2 modified, 1 staged, 3 untracked) before adding new features
Add inline documentation or architectural diagrams to reduce dependency on reading test files
Review the nopReporter and distribution tooling (goreleaser) for production readiness
